mission: CT-004: Separate claims.owned from workingSet.recentFiles in ContextPacketL1

surface_map.file: packages/opencode/src/context/packet.ts (493 lines → 492 lines after working tree diff)

latestValidation?.failedTests: Array<{ name: string; file: string; line: number }>

fields.workingSet: Array<{ path: string; freshness: string; lastEvent: string; summary: string }>
fields.recommendedNextContext: Array<{ tool: string; path: string; reason: string }>

service_interface.description: Effect Service tagged '@opencode/ContextPacket' — ZERO external consumers found

methods.getCurrentPacket: (sessionId: string, includeL2?: boolean, cwd?: string) => Effect.Effect<ContextPacket>

service_interface.consumer_finding: CRITICAL: The ContextPacket Service has ZERO external consumers in the entire codebase. No file imports or yields Packet.Service, Packet.use, or composes Packet.layer. assembleL1, assembleL2, and getCurrentPacket are ONLY referenced within packet.ts itself. The service appears to be dead code or intended for future consumer wiring.

claims_owned_mixing.description: ContextPacketL1.claims.owned mixes claim-ledger data from Coordination.getSessionReservations() with event-derived file paths from extractFilePaths(). Event-derived paths are NOT ownership claims.
flow: Line ~187-196: queries Coordination.getSessionReservations(sessionId) → returns PathReservation[] (real claim-ledger data from the coordination reservation table)

Line ~200-206: FALLBACK — if activeReservations is empty/null, queries EventStore for recentEvents and calls extractFilePaths(recentEvents) → extracts filePath from runtime events (NOT claims — just files the agent happened to reference)
Line ~209: conflictFiles = ownedFiles.filter(f => dirtyFiles.includes(f)) — conflates both sources into one array
Line ~222-225: claims = { owned: ownedFiles, conflicts: conflictFiles } — final output mixes both sources

coordination_claims.PathReservation_shape: { path: string; taskId: string; sessionId: string; status: 'reserved'|'released'|'conflicted'; createdAt: number }
coordination_claims.nature: Real ownership claims — coordination ledger records from the database

event_derived_paths.extractFilePaths_logic: Iterates RuntimeEvent[]; if ev.filePath exists and not seen, adds to result. Returns deduplicated paths in first-occurrence order.
event_derived_paths.nature: NOT ownership claims — just file paths the agent happened to reference in recent events

no_recentFiles_field.description: No 'recentFiles' field exists anywhere in the codebase. The mission description mentions 'workingSet.recentFiles' but neither ContextPacketL1 nor ContextPacketL2 has such a field. The closest analog is ContextPacketL2.workingSet (line 68-72) — an array of { path, freshness, lastEvent, summary } objects built from DuckDB ranking (rankWorkingSetFull) or FileMemory fallback.

zero_external_consumers.description: The ContextPacket Service (Packet.Service, Packet.use, Packet.layer) has ZERO external consumers anywhere in packages/opencode. The assembleL1, assembleL2, and getCurrentPacket functions are only called internally within packet.ts. The layer is never composed into any app-level Layer composition. This means any change to the interfaces has no downstream breakage risk — but the service may be dead code.
evidence: Grep for 'Packet.use|Packet.Service|Packet.layer' across entire packages/opencode → 0 matches
Grep for imports from '@/context' or relative context paths → 0 matches (barrel export at context/index.ts:7 is the only reference)

internal_references.packet.ts:85: ContextPacket.l1: ContextPacketL1 — composition into top-level packet

ContextPacketL1.note: No external files import or reference ContextPacketL1 directly

internal_references.packet.ts:459: let l2: ContextPacketL2 | undefined — variable annotation

ContextPacketL2.note: No external files import or reference ContextPacketL2 directly

ContextPacket.note: No external files import or reference ContextPacket directly

ContextPacketL1.claims.assembly: packet.ts:222-225 (built from ownedFiles + conflictFiles in assembleL1)
ContextPacketL1.claims.invalidation: context/invalidation-registry.ts:45-48 ('claims' invalidation scope: permission.asked, permission.replied, coordination.path.claimed, coordination.path.released)

campaign_predicates_claims.relationship: CONCEPTUALLY related — a flat string array, likely derived from ContextPacketL1.claims.owned. Used in state machine predicate evaluation (claims_acquired, has_claim_conflict). NOT the same object reference.
consumers: predicates.ts:185: paths.filter(p => !ctx.claims.includes(p)) — checks if claim paths exist
predicates.ts:316: ctx.claims.length > 0 — checks if any claims

push_gate_claimsVerified.relationship: CONCEPTUALLY related — a boolean gate that checks if all coordination claims are verified. In the 'push_ready → pushed' transition path.
consumers: push-gate.ts:145-146: gate evaluation — passed: ctx.claimsVerified, reason: 'Not all claims have been verified'

unrelated_claims_usages.session_instruction_claims: packages/opencode/src/session/instruction.ts:106,200,203 — a Map<string, Set<string>> tracking read-tool paths per message ID. Completely separate from coordination claims.
unrelated_claims_usages.xai_jwt_claims: packages/opencode/src/plugin/xai.ts:133 — xAI JWT token expiration check. Unrelated.

workingSet_field_specifically.definition: packet.ts:68-72 — Array<{ path, freshness, lastEvent, summary }>
workingSet_field_specifically.assembly: packet.ts:324-443 — built in assembleL2 from rankWorkingSetFull(DuckDB) with FileMemory fallback
consumers: packet.ts:410-411: topFiles = workingSet.slice(0,5).map(w => w.path) — used internally for recommendedNextContext
packet.ts:443: return { workingSet, ... } — final L2 output
workingSet_field_specifically.related_in_tools: packages/opencode/src/context/tools.ts:120,136 — formats workingSet as tool output for agent-facing context query. Builds its own workingSet from allFiles (FileMemory), NOT from ContextPacketL2.

packet_version.logic: Iterates RuntimeEvent[]. For each ev, if ev.filePath exists and not in seen Set, adds to result. Returns deduplicated paths in first-occurrence order.
packet_version.called_from: packet.ts:206 — assembleL1 fallback when Coordination.getSessionReservations returns empty

tools_version.signature: function extractFilePaths(events: readonly { readonly filePath?: string }[]): string[]
tools_version.logic: DUPLICATE implementation — same logic: extracts unique filePath values from events.
tools_version.called_from: tools.ts:444 — activePaths = extractFilePaths(recentEvents) for context tools output

extractFilePaths.note: Two near-identical extractFilePaths functions exist in two files. Both extract event-derived file paths — neither produces ownership claims.

PathReservation_shape.taskId: string — the task that holds the reservation

Coordination_getSessionReservations.implementation: Queries CoordinationReservationTable via DatabaseAdapter. Selects rows where sessionId matches and status is active. Maps via reservationRowToType().

getCurrentPacket.called_by: NOT called externally — only available through the service interface
getCurrentPacket.logic: Always calls assembleL1. If includeL2, caches and returns L2. Assembles final ContextPacket { l1, l2?, packetVersion: 1 }.

closest_analogs: ContextPacketL2.workingSet (packet.ts:68-72) — ranked working set files from DuckDB ranking
FileMemory.getAll() — returns all file memory entries, used as fallback for working set
extractFilePaths output — event-derived file paths, used as fallback for claims.owned
PredicateContext.claims (predicates.ts:30) — flat string[] used in campaign predicates
recentFiles_field_search.recommendation: If the mission intends to add a 'recentFiles' field to workingSet, the natural location is ContextPacketL2.workingSet as an additional subfield or a separate top-level field. The 'recentFiles' could be derived from EventStore's most recent events (like extractFilePaths does) without conflating them with ownership claims.
